# Architektur-Spezifikation: Industrieller Badge-Generator V6.0

## Executive Summary

Diese Spezifikation definiert die vollständige technische Architektur eines modularen Badge-Generators mit Echtzeit-Vorschau, asynchronem Export-Modul und vollständig entkoppeltem CSS-Theming. Die Architektur folgt strikten OOP-Prinzipien und implementiert ein bidirektionales Traceability-System zwischen DOM, State und Styling.

---

## 1. Architektonische Säulen

### 1.1 Modulare OOP-Logik: Klassenhierarchie

```
BadgeGeneratorApplication (Hauptklasse)
├── StateManager (Singleton)
│   ├── state: Object (zentraler Zustandsbaum)
│   ├── observers: WeakMap<Function, Set<string>>
│   └── methods:
│       ├── setState(path, value, options)
│       ├── getState(path)
│       ├── subscribe(callback, watchPaths)
│       └── batch(callback)
│
├── DOMController
│   ├── elementRegistry: Map<string, HTMLElement>
│   ├── hookPrefix: "bdg" (data-bdg-*)
│   └── methods:
│       ├── registerElements(scopeSelector)
│       ├── getElement(hookName)
│       ├── attachListener(hookName, eventType, handler)
│       └── validateDOM()
│
├── CanvasRenderer
│   ├── canvas: HTMLCanvasElement
│   ├── context: CanvasRenderingContext2D
│   ├── renderQueue: Array<RenderTask>
│   └── methods:
│       ├── render(stateSnapshot)
│       ├── drawBackground(config)
│       ├── drawImage(imageData, transform)
│       ├── drawText(textData, styling)
│       └── clearCanvas()
│
├── ImageProcessor
│   ├── uploadedImages: Map<string, ImageData>
│   ├── maxDimensions: {width, height}
│   └── methods:
│       ├── processUpload(file)
│       ├── validateImage(file)
│       ├── resizeIfNeeded(image, maxDims)
│       └── generateThumbnail(image)
│
├── ExportModule
│   ├── exportFormats: ["png", "jpeg", "webp"]
│   ├── exportQueue: AsyncQueue
│   └── methods:
│       ├── exportAsPNG(quality, scale)
│       ├── exportAsJPEG(quality, scale)
│       ├── batchExport(configs)
│       └── downloadFile(blob, filename)
│
└── ThemeEngine
    ├── activeTheme: string
    ├── themeRegistry: Map<string, ThemeConfig>
    └── methods:
        ├── loadTheme(themeName)
        ├── applyTheme(themeConfig)
        ├── registerCustomTheme(name, config)
        └── syncCSSVariables(stateChanges)
```

### 1.2 Traceability-System: Namenskonventionen

#### Data-JS Hooks (Logik-Anker)

**Namensschema:** `data-bdg-{kategorie}-{funktion}`

| Kategorie | Funktion | Beispiel | Zweck |
|-----------|----------|----------|-------|
| `action` | Nutzerinteraktion | `data-bdg-action-upload` | Triggert asynchrone Upload-Logik |
| `action` | Export | `data-bdg-action-export-png` | Startet PNG-Export-Pipeline |
| `input` | Wert-Eingabe | `data-bdg-input-rotation` | Bindet an `state.transform.rotation` |
| `input` | Text-Feld | `data-bdg-input-name` | Bindet an `state.content.name` |
| `display` | Vorschau | `data-bdg-display-canvas` | Ziel für Canvas-Rendering |
| `display` | Status | `data-bdg-display-status` | Zeigt asynchrone Operation an |
| `toggle` | Boolean | `data-bdg-toggle-grid` | Schaltet `state.ui.showGrid` |
| `range` | Slider | `data-bdg-range-scale` | Bindet an `state.transform.scale` |

**Interne Mapping-Konvention:**

```
DOM-Hook → StateManager-Pfad → Event-Handler-Methode

data-bdg-input-rotation 
  → state.transform.rotation 
  → DOMController.handleRotationInput()
```

#### Data-Sync Attributes (CSS-Variable-Bridge)

**Namensschema:** `data-sync-{css-var-name}`

| Attribute | CSS-Variable | State-Pfad | Zweck |
|-----------|--------------|------------|-------|
| `data-sync-primary-color` | `--bdg-primary` | `state.theme.primaryColor` | Haupt-Akzentfarbe |
| `data-sync-rotation` | `--bdg-rotation` | `state.transform.rotation` | Live-CSS-Rotation |
| `data-sync-scale` | `--bdg-scale` | `state.transform.scale` | Live-CSS-Skalierung |
| `data-sync-grid-size` | `--bdg-grid-size` | `state.ui.gridSize` | Raster-Abstände |

**Synchronisations-Mechanismus:**

```
StateManager.setState() 
  → detectSyncableChanges(changedPaths)
  → ThemeEngine.syncCSSVariables(changes)
  → document.documentElement.style.setProperty()
```

### 1.3 CSS-Theme-Engine: Root-Variablen

#### Farb-Palette (Vollständig austauschbar)

```
/* Primärfarben */
--bdg-primary: #2563eb
--bdg-primary-hover: #1d4ed8
--bdg-primary-rgb: 37, 99, 235

/* Sekundärfarben */
--bdg-secondary: #64748b
--bdg-accent: #f59e0b
--bdg-danger: #ef4444
--bdg-success: #10b981

/* Neutralfarben */
--bdg-bg-primary: #ffffff
--bdg-bg-secondary: #f8fafc
--bdg-bg-tertiary: #e2e8f0
--bdg-text-primary: #0f172a
--bdg-text-secondary: #475569
--bdg-text-muted: #94a3b8

/* Status-Farben */
--bdg-status-idle: #3b82f6
--bdg-status-processing: #f59e0b
--bdg-status-success: #10b981
--bdg-status-error: #ef4444
```

#### Dimensionen & Layout

```
/* Abstände */
--bdg-spacing-xs: 0.25rem
--bdg-spacing-sm: 0.5rem
--bdg-spacing-md: 1rem
--bdg-spacing-lg: 1.5rem
--bdg-spacing-xl: 2rem

/* Canvas-Dimensionen */
--bdg-canvas-width: 800px
--bdg-canvas-height: 600px
--bdg-canvas-aspect-ratio: 4/3

/* Kontrollelemente */
--bdg-control-height: 2.5rem
--bdg-control-radius: 0.5rem
--bdg-control-border-width: 1px
```

#### Typografie

```
--bdg-font-family-base: 'Inter', sans-serif
--bdg-font-family-mono: 'Fira Code', monospace

--bdg-font-size-xs: 0.75rem
--bdg-font-size-sm: 0.875rem
--bdg-font-size-base: 1rem
--bdg-font-size-lg: 1.125rem
--bdg-font-size-xl: 1.25rem
--bdg-font-size-2xl: 1.5rem

--bdg-font-weight-normal: 400
--bdg-font-weight-medium: 500
--bdg-font-weight-semibold: 600
--bdg-font-weight-bold: 700

--bdg-line-height-tight: 1.25
--bdg-line-height-normal: 1.5
--bdg-line-height-relaxed: 1.75
```

#### Effekte & Animationen

```
/* Schatten */
--bdg-shadow-sm: 0 1px 2px 0 rgb(0 0 0 / 0.05)
--bdg-shadow-md: 0 4px 6px -1px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)
--bdg-shadow-lg: 0 10px 15px -3px rgb(0 0 0 / 0.1)

/* Übergänge */
--bdg-transition-fast: 150ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)
--bdg-transition-base: 250ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)
--bdg-transition-slow: 350ms cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1)

/* Transformationen */
--bdg-rotation: 0deg
--bdg-scale: 1
--bdg-translate-x: 0px
--bdg-translate-y: 0px
```

#### Theme-Varianten

```
/* Dark Theme Override */
[data-theme="dark"] {
  --bdg-bg-primary: #0f172a
  --bdg-bg-secondary: #1e293b
  --bdg-bg-tertiary: #334155
  --bdg-text-primary: #f8fafc
  --bdg-text-secondary: #cbd5e1
  --bdg-text-muted: #64748b
}

/* High Contrast Theme */
[data-theme="high-contrast"] {
  --bdg-primary: #000000
  --bdg-bg-primary: #ffffff
  --bdg-text-primary: #000000
  --bdg-control-border-width: 2px
}
```

### 1.4 State-Management: Zentraler Zustandsbaum

```
state = {
  content: {
    name: string,
    title: string,
    subtitle: string,
    customFields: Map<string, string>
  },
  
  image: {
    uploaded: boolean,
    data: ImageData | null,
    originalDimensions: {width, height},
    position: {x, y},
    fileName: string
  },
  
  transform: {
    rotation: number (0-360),
    scale: number (0.1-3.0),
    flipHorizontal: boolean,
    flipVertical: boolean,
    snapEnabled: boolean,
    snapAngle: number (15 | 30 | 45 | 90)
  },
  
  theme: {
    activeName: string,
    primaryColor: string,
    backgroundColor: string,
    textColor: string,
    customOverrides: Map<string, string>
  },
  
  ui: {
    showGrid: boolean,
    gridSize: number,
    showRulers: boolean,
    showPreview: boolean,
    zoom: number (0.25-4.0),
    activePanel: string
  },
  
  export: {
    format: "png" | "jpeg" | "webp",
    quality: number (0-100),
    scale: number (1-4),
    includeMetadata: boolean,
    processing: boolean,
    progress: number (0-100)
  },
  
  validation: {
    errors: Map<string, ValidationError>,
    warnings: Map<string, string>,
    isValid: boolean
  },
  
  meta: {
    version: string,
    lastModified: timestamp,
    sessionId: string,
    undoStack: Array<StateSnapshot>,
    redoStack: Array<StateSnapshot>
  }
}
```

#### Render-Trigger-Mechanismus

**Optimistic UI Updates:**

```
Logischer Ablauf:

1. Event-Handler wird aufgerufen
   → DOMController.handleRotationInput(event)

2. Validation-Layer prüft Eingabe
   → validateRotation(value) → {valid, sanitized, errors}

3. State-Update mit Batch-Optimization
   → StateManager.batch(() => {
       setState('transform.rotation', sanitizedValue)
       setState('validation.errors', computedErrors)
     })

4. Observer-Pattern triggert Subscriptions
   → StateManager notifiziert registrierte Callbacks
   → CanvasRenderer.render() [wenn transform.* geändert]
   → ThemeEngine.syncCSSVariables() [wenn sync-Attribute betroffen]
   → DOMController.updateDisplayElements() [wenn display-* betroffen]

5. Render-Queue priorisiert Tasks
   → Kritisch: Canvas-Render (60fps-Target)
   → Normal: CSS-Updates (sofort)
   → Low: Status-Anzeigen (debounced 200ms)
```

---

## 2. Deliverables: Schritt-für-Schritt-Architektur

### Phase 1: Das Blueprint (Strukturelles Design)

#### Mermaid Sequence Diagram: Kern-Logik-Flow

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant DOM as DOMController
    participant State as StateManager
    participant Img as ImageProcessor
    participant Canvas as CanvasRenderer
    participant Export as ExportModule
    participant Theme as ThemeEngine

    %% Image Upload Flow
    User->>DOM: Klick auf Upload-Button
    DOM->>DOM: Validiere data-bdg-action-upload
    DOM->>Img: processUpload(file)
    Img->>Img: validateImage(file)
    alt Validation erfolgreich
        Img->>Img: resizeIfNeeded(image)
        Img->>State: setState('image.data', processedImage)
        State->>State: Trigger observers für 'image.*'
        State->>Canvas: render(stateSnapshot)
        Canvas->>Canvas: drawBackground()
        Canvas->>Canvas: drawImage(imageData, transform)
        Canvas->>DOM: updateCanvas(renderedData)
        State->>Theme: syncCSSVariables({image.uploaded: true})
        Theme->>DOM: Aktualisiere data-sync-* Elemente
    else Validation fehlgeschlagen
        Img->>State: setState('validation.errors', errorMap)
        State->>DOM: updateDisplayElements(errors)
    end

    %% Transformation Flow
    User->>DOM: Ändert Rotation-Slider
    DOM->>State: setState('transform.rotation', newValue)
    State->>State: Prüfe snapEnabled
    alt Snap aktiviert
        State->>State: Berechne nächsten Snap-Point
        State->>State: setState('transform.rotation', snappedValue)
    end
    State->>Canvas: render(stateSnapshot)
    Canvas->>Canvas: drawImage(imageData, updatedTransform)
    State->>Theme: syncCSSVariables({rotation: snappedValue})
    Theme->>DOM: Setze --bdg-rotation CSS-Variable

    %% Export Flow
    User->>DOM: Klick auf Export-PNG-Button
    DOM->>State: setState('export.processing', true)
    State->>Export: exportAsPNG(quality, scale)
    Export->>Canvas: render(highResSnapshot)
    Canvas-->>Export: Canvas-Blob
    Export->>Export: Konvertiere zu PNG
    Export->>Export: Füge Metadata hinzu
    loop Progress-Updates
        Export->>State: setState('export.progress', percentage)
        State->>DOM: Aktualisiere Progress-Bar
    end
    Export->>Export: downloadFile(blob, filename)
    Export->>State: setState('export.processing', false)
    State->>DOM: Reset UI-Status
```

#### Traceability-Matrix: UI-Action → Logik → Styling

| UI-Element | data-bdg Hook | Event | JS-Methode | State-Pfad | CSS-Variable | Beschreibung |
|------------|---------------|-------|------------|------------|--------------|--------------|
| Upload-Button | `action-upload` | click | `ImageProcessor.processUpload()` | `image.data` | `--bdg-image-loaded` | Triggert asynchronen Upload-Prozess |
| Rotation-Slider | `range-rotation` | input | `DOMController.handleRotationInput()` | `transform.rotation` | `--bdg-rotation` | Echtzeit-Rotation mit Snap-Logik |
| Scale-Slider | `range-scale` | input | `DOMController.handleScaleInput()` | `transform.scale` | `--bdg-scale` | Skalierung mit Min/Max-Bounds |
| Name-Input | `input-name` | input | `DOMController.handleTextInput()` | `content.name` | - | Debounced State-Update (300ms) |
| Grid-Toggle | `toggle-grid` | change | `DOMController.handleToggle()` | `ui.showGrid` | `--bdg-grid-opacity` | Boolean-State mit CSS-Visibility |
| Theme-Select | `input-theme` | change | `ThemeEngine.loadTheme()` | `theme.activeName` | ALL `--bdg-*` | Kompletter Theme-Swap |
| Export-PNG | `action-export-png` | click | `ExportModule.exportAsPNG()` | `export.processing` | `--bdg-export-status` | Async Export mit Progress |
| Snap-Toggle | `toggle-snap` | change | `DOMController.handleSnapToggle()` | `transform.snapEnabled` | - | Aktiviert magnetische Rotation |
| Canvas-Display | `display-canvas` | - | `CanvasRenderer.render()` | - | - | Render-Target für Canvas-Output |
| Status-Display | `display-status` | - | `DOMController.updateStatus()` | `export.progress` | `--bdg-status-color` | Zeigt async Operation Status |
| Undo-Button | `action-undo` | click | `StateManager.undo()` | `meta.undoStack` | - | Popt letzten State vom Stack |
| Redo-Button | `action-redo` | click | `StateManager.redo()` | `meta.redoStack` | - | Restored gepoppten State |

### Phase 2: Fail-Safe & Logik-Spezifikationen

#### Error-Boundary-Logik

**Defensive Programmierung auf drei Ebenen:**

**Ebene 1: DOM-Validierung beim Initialisieren**

```
Logischer Ablauf im DOMController.validateDOM():

1. Iteriere über alle erforderlichen data-bdg Hooks
   → Definiert in requiredHooks: Set<string>

2. Für jeden Hook:
   → query = `[data-bdg-${hook}]`
   → element = document.querySelector(query)
   
3. Wenn Element fehlt:
   → Erstelle DetailedError mit:
     - Fehlertyp: "MISSING_DOM_ELEMENT"
     - Hook-Name: hook
     - Erwarteter Selector: query
     - Kontext: "Initialization Phase"
   → Füge zu errorRegistry hinzu
   → Logge Warning in Console mit Stack-Trace

4. Wenn kritischer Hook fehlt:
   → Kritische Hooks: action-upload, display-canvas, action-export-png
   → Werfe FatalInitializationError
   → Zeige Fallback-UI mit Recovery-Optionen
   → Disable alle Interaktionen

5. Wenn nicht-kritischer Hook fehlt:
   → Markiere Feature als "degraded"
   → Setze Feature-Flag: features.get(hookName).enabled = false
   → UI zeigt "Feature nicht verfügbar"-Badge
   → App läuft weiter mit reduzierter Funktionalität
```

**Ebene 2: Async-Operation Error Handling**

```
Async-Wrapper-Pattern für alle Promises:

function safeAsync(asyncOperation, fallbackValue, errorContext) {
  Logik:
  
  1. Starte try-catch-Block
  
  2. Führe asyncOperation() aus
  
  3. Bei Erfolg:
     → return result
  
  4. Bei Fehler:
     → Klassifiziere Error-Type:
       - NetworkError → Retry-Logik (3x mit exponential backoff)
       - ValidationError → Zeige User-Feedback, no retry
       - FileSystemError → Prüfe Permissions, zeige Hilfe-Dialog
       - UnknownError → Logge zu Sentry/Error-Service
     
     → Update State:
       setState('validation.errors', new Map([
         [errorContext, {
           type: error.constructor.name,
           message: userFriendlyMessage,
           timestamp: Date.now(),
           recoverable: isRecoverable(error)
         }]
       ]))
     
     → Wenn recoverable:
       → Zeige Retry-Button in UI
       → Store operation in retryQueue
     
     → Wenn non-recoverable:
       → return fallbackValue
       → Disable betroffene UI-Kontrolle
       → Logge permanent zu errorRegistry
  
  5. Finally-Block:
     → Cleanup: Revoke Object-URLs, clear Timeouts
     → Reset Loading-States
     → Emit Telemetrie-Event
}
```

**Ebene 3: State-Integrity-Checks**

```
StateManager.setState() mit Guards:

1. Pre-Validation:
   → Prüfe ob path existiert in state-Schema
   → Prüfe Typ-Kompatibilität (string, number, boolean, etc.)
   → Prüfe Range-Constraints (z.B. rotation: 0-360)

2. Bei Validation-Fehler:
   → Reject State-Change
   → Werfe StateValidationError
   → State bleibt unverändert
   → UI zeigt vorherigen validen Wert

3. Post-Update Integrity-Check:
   → Prüfe referentielle Integrität (z.B. image.uploaded === true, aber image.data === null)
   → Bei Inkonsistenz:
     → Rollback zu letztem konsistenten State
     → Logge Warning
     → Trigger Auto-Correction-Routine

4. Undo/Redo-Safety:
   → Validiere State-Snapshot vor Push zu undoStack
   → Begrenze Stack-Size (default: 50 Einträge)
   → Bei Stack-Overflow: Verwerfe ältesten Eintrag (FIFO)
```

#### Async-Execution-Flow: Promise-Chain für PNG-Export

**Detaillierte Ablaufbeschreibung ohne Code:**

**Phase 1: Pre-Export Validation (synchron)**

```
1. ExportModule.exportAsPNG() wird aufgerufen
   → Parameter: quality (0-100), scale (1-4)

2. Validiere Parameter:
   → quality in Range? → Clamp zu [0, 100]
   → scale in Range? → Clamp zu [1, 4]
   → Canvas existiert? → Werfe FatalError falls nicht

3. Prüfe State-Konsistenz:
   → Ist image.data vorhanden?
   → Ist Canvas korrekt gerendert?
   → Wenn nein: Trigger CanvasRenderer.render() synchron

4. Setze UI-Status:
   → setState('export.processing', true)
   → setState('export.progress', 0)
   → Disable Export-Buttons (prevent duplicate clicks)
```

**Phase 2: Async Canvas-Processing**

```
5. Erstelle High-Res-Canvas (Promise 1):
   Logik:
   → Klone Original-Canvas
   → Multipliziere Dimensionen mit scale-Faktor
   → targetWidth = originalWidth * scale
   → targetHeight = originalHeight * scale
   → Erstelle neues Canvas-Element (off-screen)
   → Setze Dimensionen
   
   Progress-Update:
   → setState('export.progress', 10)

6. Re-Render mit höherer Auflösung (Promise 2):
   Logik:
   → Hole aktuellen State-Snapshot
   → Skaliere alle Dimensionen (Schrift, Bilder, Abstände) mit scale-Faktor
   → Rufe CanvasRenderer.render(scaledSnapshot, highResCanvas)
   → Verwende anti-aliasing: imageSmoothingQuality = 'high'
   
   Progress-Update:
   → setState('export.progress', 40)
```

**Phase 3: Blob-Conversion**

```
7. Canvas-zu-Blob-Konvertierung (Promise 3):
   Logik:
   → Rufe canvas.toBlob() mit Parametern:
     - type: 'image/png'
     - quality: quality / 100
   → toBlob() ist async und gibt Callback
   → Wrap in Promise für Chain
   
   Progress-Update:
   → setState('export.progress', 70)

8. Metadata-Injection (Promise 4):
   Logik:
   → Falls export.includeMetadata === true:
     → Lese Blob als ArrayBuffer
     → Parse PNG-Chunks (Binary-Format)
     → Erstelle tEXt-Chunk mit Metadata:
       - "Software": "Badge Generator V6.0"
       - "CreationDate": ISO-8601-Timestamp
       - "Author": state.content.name
       - "Configuration": JSON.stringify(state)
     → Injiziere Chunk vor IEND-Chunk
     → Erstelle neuen Blob aus modifiziertem Buffer
   
   Progress-Update:
   → setState('export.progress', 90)
```

**Phase 4: Download-Trigger**

```
9. File-Download (Promise 5):
   Logik:
   → Erstelle Object-URL: URL.createObjectURL(finalBlob)
   → Generiere Filename:
     - Pattern: "badge_{name}_{timestamp}.png"
     - Sanitize name: Entferne Sonderzeichen
     - Timestamp: YYYYMMDD_HHmmss
   → Erstelle temporäres <a>-Element
   → Setze href zu Object-URL
   → Setze download-Attribute zu Filename
   → Trigger programmatischen Click
   
   Progress-Update:
   → setState('export.progress', 100)

10. Cleanup:
    Logik:
    → Revoke Object-URL (Speicher freigeben)
    → Entferne temporäres <a>-Element
    → Lösche High-Res-Canvas
    → Reset State:
      - setState('export.processing', false)
      - setState('export.progress', 0)
    → Re-enable Export-Buttons
    → Zeige Success-Toast: "Export erfolgreich"
```

**Error-Handling in jeder Phase:**

```
Bei Fehler in Promise-Chain:

→ Catch-Block wird aufgerufen
→ Bestimme Phase anhand export.progress:
  - 0-20: Validation/Setup-Error
  - 20-50: Rendering-Error
  - 50-80: Conversion-Error
  - 80-100: Download-Error

→ Spezifische Error-Messages:
  - Validation: "Ungültige Export-Parameter"
  - Rendering: "Canvas-Rendering fehlgeschlagen"
  - Conversion: "Bild-Konvertierung fehlgeschlagen"
  - Download: "Download konnte nicht gestartet werden"

→ Recovery-Aktionen:
  - Alle: Reset export.processing zu false
  - Rendering-Error: Trigger CanvasRenderer.clearCanvas(), dann re-render
  - Download-Error: Zeige "Manueller Download"-Button mit Blob-URL

→ Logging:
  - Console-Error mit Stack-Trace
  - Sentry/Error-Service-Report
  - User-Event-Log: "Export failed at ${phase}"
```

#### Mathematische Snapping-Logik

**Algorithmus für magnetische Rotations-Punkte:**

**Input-Parameter:**

```
- currentRotation: number (0-360, kontinuierlicher Wert vom Slider)
- snapEnabled: boolean (State: transform.snapEnabled)
- snapAngle: number (State: transform.snapAngle, z.B. 15, 30, 45, 90)
- snapThreshold: number (Konstante: 5 Grad, "magnetische Reichweite")
```

**Algorithmus-Beschreibung:**

```
Funktion: calculateSnappedRotation(currentRotation)

1. Früher Exit bei deaktiviertem Snap:
   → Wenn snapEnabled === false:
     → return currentRotation (unverändert)

2. Normalisierung des Eingabewerts:
   → normalized = currentRotation % 360
   → Wenn normalized < 0: normalized += 360
   → Stelle sicher: 0 ≤ normalized < 360

3. Berechne nächstgelegenen Snap-Point:
   → snapPoints = generiere Array [0, snapAngle, 2*snapAngle, ..., 360-snapAngle]
   → Beispiel bei snapAngle=45: [0, 45, 90, 135, 180, 225, 270, 315]
   
   → Für jeden snapPoint in snapPoints:
     → distance = min(
         abs(normalized - snapPoint),
         360 - abs(normalized - snapPoint)  // Wrap-around-Distanz
       )
     → Wenn distance < minDistance:
       → minDistance = distance
       → nearestSnapPoint = snapPoint

4. Entscheide: Snap oder Freie Rotation:
   → Wenn minDistance <= snapThreshold:
     → return nearestSnapPoint (SNAP!)
   → Sonst:
     → return normalized (freie Bewegung)

5. Visuelle Feedback-Logik (parallel):
   → Wenn minDistance <= snapThreshold * 2:
     → Zeige visuellen Snap-Indikator:
       - Ändere Cursor zu "magnet"-Icon
       - Highlight Snap-Point in UI
       - Kurze Vibration (falls Haptic-API verfügbar)
       - Setze CSS-Variable: --bdg-snap-active: 1
   → Sonst:
     → Normaler Cursor
     → --bdg-snap-active: 0
```

**Erweiterte Logik: Multi-Achsen-Snap:**

```
Für kombiniertes Snap bei Rotation + Skalierung:

Funktion: calculateCombinedSnap(rotation, scale)

1. Rotation-Snap (wie oben):
   → snappedRotation = calculateSnappedRotation(rotation)

2. Scale-Snap (ähnliche Logik):
   → scaleSnapPoints = [0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0, 2.5, 3.0]
   → scaleSnapThreshold = 0.1
   → nearestScaleSnap = findNearest(scale, scaleSnapPoints)
   → Wenn abs(scale - nearestScaleSnap) <= scaleSnapThreshold:
     → snappedScale = nearestScaleSnap
   → Sonst:
     → snappedScale = scale

3. Kombiniertes Feedback:
   → Wenn BEIDE gesnapped:
     → Zeige "Perfekt ausgerichtet



## Response:
22.1.2026, 15:03:13

"-Badge
     → Setze --bdg-perfect-snap: 1
   → Sonst:
     → --bdg-perfect-snap: 0

4. Return:
   → { rotation: snappedRotation, scale: snappedScale, isSnapped: boolean }
```

**Performance-Optimierung:**

```
Problem: Snap-Berechnung bei jedem Input-Event (potenziell 60+ fps)

Lösung: Debouncing + RequestAnimationFrame

Logik:

1. Input-Event wird gefeuert:
   → Speichere rawValue in temporärer Variable
   → Wenn kein AnimationFrame geplant:
     → requestAnimationFrame(performSnapCalculation)

2. performSnapCalculation (einmal pro Frame):
   → Hole aktuellsten rawValue
   → Führe calculateSnappedRotation() aus
   → Update State NUR wenn snappedValue !== previousValue
   → Reset AnimationFrame-Flag

Resultat:
→ Maximale Rechenfrequenz: 60 Hz (1 Berechnung pro Frame)
→ Keine unnötigen State-Updates bei identischen Werten
→ Smooth Animation trotz Snapping
```

### Phase 3: Cold-Eye-Review (Selbst-Validierung)

#### Identifizierte Schwachstellen

**Schwachstelle 1: Memory-Management bei High-Res-Export**

**Problem:**
- Bei scale=4 und großen Canvas-Dimensionen (z.B. 800x600 Original):
  - High-Res-Canvas: 3200x2400 Pixel
  - RGBA-Buffer: 3200 * 2400 * 4 Bytes = ~30 MB
  - Zusätzlich: Object-URLs, Blob-Kopien, Metadata-Buffer
  - Gesamt-Memory-Spike: 60-100 MB pro Export
- Bei schnellen wiederholten Exporten:
  - Garbage-Collector kommt nicht hinterher
  - Memory-Leak durch nicht-revokete Object-URLs
  - Potenzieller Browser-Crash auf mobilen Geräten

**Mitigation-Strategien:**

```
1. Explizite Memory-Freigabe:
   → Erstelle MemoryManager-Klasse mit:
     - trackAllocation(resource, size) → registriert Ressource
     - releaseResource(resource) → Cleanup + Deregistrierung
     - getTotalMemoryUsage() → Summe aller tracked Resources
   
   → Bei Export-Start:
     - Prüfe getTotalMemoryUsage()
     - Wenn > threshold (z.B. 200MB):
       - Trigger manuelle Garbage-Collection (wenn verfügbar)
       - Zeige Warning: "Hoher Speicherverbrauch, Export kann langsam sein"
   
   → Bei Export-Ende (finally-Block):
     - Explizite Cleanup-Reihenfolge:
       1. URL.revokeObjectURL(blobURL)
       2. highResCanvas.width = 0; highResCanvas.height = 0
       3. highResCanvas = null
       4. blob = null
     - Force null-Assignments für GC-Hints

2. Chunked-Processing für sehr große Exports:
   → Wenn targetDimensions > 4000x4000:
     - Teile Canvas in Tiles (z.B. 1000x1000)
     - Rendere und exportiere Tiles sequenziell
     - Füge Tiles in Worker-Thread zusammen
     - Reduziert Peak-Memory um 70-80%

3. Export-Queue mit Concurrency-Limit:
   → Maximal 1 gleichzeitiger Export
   → Weitere Export-Requests gehen in Queue
   → UI zeigt Position in Queue
   → Verhindert Memory-Spikes durch parallele Exports
```

**Schwachstelle 2: Event-Listener-Overhead bei vielen DOM-Elementen**

**Problem:**
- Bei komplexer UI mit 50+ interaktiven Elementen:
  - 50 event listeners für 'input'-Events
  - 30 event listeners für 'change'-Events
  - 20 event listeners für 'click'-Events
  - Jeder Listener erstellt Closure → Memory-Overhead
- Bei dynamisch generierten Elementen (z.B. Custom-Fields):
  - Listener werden nicht automatisch entfernt
  - Memory-Leak bei Add/Remove-Zyklen
- Performance-Impact:
  - Event-Delegation fehlt → O(n) Lookup bei jedem Event

**Mitigation-Strategien:**

```
1. Event-Delegation-Pattern:
   → Statt individuelle Listener auf jedem Element:
     - EIN Listener auf Parent-Container
     - Event-Bubbling nutzen
     - Target-Element via event.target.closest('[data-bdg-*]')
   
   → Beispiel-Logik:
     - document.addEventListener('input', globalInputHandler)
     - globalInputHandler prüft:
       - hook = event.target.dataset.bdgInput
       - Wenn hook existiert: rufe entsprechende Handler-Methode
   
   → Vorteile:
     - Konstante Memory-Nutzung (O(1) Listener)
     - Automatisches Handling dynamischer Elemente
     - Einfacheres Cleanup

2. Listener-Registry mit Auto-Cleanup:
   → DOMController führt WeakMap mit Listener-Referenzen:
     - listenerRegistry: WeakMap<HTMLElement, Set<Listener>>
   
   → Bei attachListener():
     - Speichere Listener in WeakMap
     - Setze MutationObserver auf Parent
   
   → MutationObserver-Callback:
     - Bei removedNodes:
       - Hole Listener aus WeakMap
       - Rufe removeEventListener() für alle
       - WeakMap-Entry wird automatisch GC'd
   
   → Eliminiert Memory-Leaks bei dynamischen Elementen

3. Passive Event-Listeners für Scroll/Touch:
   → Alle scroll-, touchmove-, wheel-Events mit {passive: true}
   → Verhindert, dass Browser auf preventDefault() warten muss
   → Verbessert Scroll-Performance um 30-40%
```

**Schwachstelle 3: Browser-spezifische Canvas-Limitierungen**

**Problem:**
- Canvas-Größen-Limits variieren stark:
  - Chrome/Edge: Max 32767x32767 Pixel
  - Firefox: Max 16384x16384 Pixel (abhängig von gfx.max-texture-size)
  - Safari iOS: Max 4096x4096 Pixel (abhängig von RAM)
- Bei Überschreitung:
  - canvas.toBlob() liefert null ohne Error
  - Oder: Browser-Crash ohne Warnung
- Canvas-Kontext-Limitierungen:
  - Maximale Anzahl aktiver Kontexte: ~16-32
  - Bei Überschreitung: getContext() gibt null zurück

**Mitigation-Strategien:**

```
1. Feature-Detection beim App-Start:
   → Erstelle CanvasCapabilityDetector:
     - Methode: detectMaxCanvasSize()
       - Binäre Suche um maximale Dimension zu finden
       - Testweise erstelle Canvas mit steigenden Größen
       - Prüfe ob getContext('2d') !== null
       - Speichere gefundenes Maximum in capabilities.maxCanvasSize
   
   → Export-Validation nutzt detected Maximum:
     - Bei scale-Auswahl:
       - targetSize = originalSize * scale
       - Wenn targetSize > capabilities.maxCanvasSize:
         - Zeige Warning
         - Reduziere scale automatisch
         - Oder: Biete Tile-Export an

2. Graceful Degradation:
   → Fallback-Chain bei Canvas-Fehlern:
     1. Versuche High-Res-Export (scale=4)
     2. Bei Fehler: Retry mit scale=2
     3. Bei erneutem Fehler: Fallback zu scale=1
     4. Bei immer noch Fehler: Nutze SVG-Export
   
   → User-Feedback:
     - "Hohe Auflösung nicht verfügbar auf diesem Gerät"
     - "Export mit reduzierter Qualität wird fortgesetzt"
     - Zeige tatsächlich verwendeten Scale-Faktor

3. Context-Pooling:
   → Limitiere Anzahl gleichzeitiger Canvas-Kontexte:
     - ContextPool-Klasse mit maxSize=8
     - Acquire/Release-Pattern
   
   → Bei acquire():
     - Wenn pool.size < maxSize:
       - Erstelle neuen Context, füge zu Pool
     - Sonst:
       - Warte bis Context released wird (Promise-Queue)
   
   → Bei release():
     - Canvas-Content clearen (nicht Context destroyen)
     - Markiere als "available" in Pool
     - Resolve nächste wartende Promise
   
   → Verhindert Context-Erschöpfung zuverlässig
```

---

## 3. Zusätzliche Architektur-Spezifikationen

### 3.1 Plugin-Architektur für Erweiterbarkeit

**Interface-Definition:**

```
Plugin-System-Struktur:

Interface: BadgePlugin
  - name: string
  - version: string
  - dependencies: Array<string>
  - lifecycle: {
      onInit(app: BadgeGeneratorApplication): void
      onStateChange(path: string, value: any): void
      onBeforeExport(config: ExportConfig): ExportConfig
      onAfterRender(canvas: HTMLCanvasElement): void
      onDestroy(): void
    }
  - capabilities: {
      providesRenderer: boolean
      providesExporter: boolean
      extendsState: boolean
    }
  - registerHooks(): Map<string, Function>

Plugin-Lifecycle:

1. Registration-Phase:
   → PluginManager.register(plugin)
   → Validiere Interface-Compliance
   → Prüfe Dependencies (sind required Plugins geladen?)
   → Füge zu pluginRegistry hinzu

2. Initialization-Phase:
   → Sortiere Plugins nach Dependency-Graph (topologische Sortierung)
   → Für jedes Plugin (in Reihenfolge):
     - Rufe plugin.onInit(app)
     - Plugin kann:
       - State erweitern: app.state.registerNamespace('myPlugin', {...})
       - Hooks registrieren: plugin.registerHooks()
       - UI-Elemente injizieren: app.dom.injectElement(...)

3. Runtime-Phase:
   → Bei StateChange:
     - Notifiziere alle Plugins via onStateChange()
     - Plugins können State-Changes abonnieren
   
   → Bei Export:
     - Rufe onBeforeExport() für alle Exporter-Plugins
     - Plugins können ExportConfig modifizieren
   
   → Bei Render:
     - Rufe onAfterRender() für alle Renderer-Plugins
     - Plugins können Post-Processing durchführen

4. Teardown-Phase:
   → Bei App-Destroy:
     - Reverse-Order durch Plugins
     - Rufe onDestroy() für Cleanup
```

### 3.2 Accessibility-Spezifikation

**WCAG 2.1 Level AA Compliance:**

```
1. Keyboard-Navigation:
   → Alle interaktiven Elemente müssen fokussierbar sein
   → Tab-Reihenfolge muss logisch sein (tabindex=-1 nur bei explizitem Bedarf)
   → Keyboard-Shortcuts:
     - Ctrl+Z: Undo
     - Ctrl+Shift+Z: Redo
     - Ctrl+E: Export
     - Ctrl+G: Toggle Grid
     - Escape: Close Modals
   → Focus-Indicator:
     - Min. 2px Outline
     - Kontrast: 3:1 zu Hintergrund
     - CSS: outline: 2px solid var(--bdg-primary)

2. Screen-Reader-Support:
   → Alle data-bdg-Elemente benötigen aria-label:
     - data-bdg-action-upload → aria-label="Bild hochladen"
     - data-bdg-range-rotation → aria-label="Rotation in Grad" aria-valuenow aria-valuemin aria-valuemax
   → Live-Regions für Status-Updates:
     - Export-Progress: aria-live="polite" aria-atomic="true"
     - Error-Messages: aria-live="assertive" role="alert"
   → Semantic HTML:
     - <button> statt <div onclick>
     - <label> für alle Inputs
     - <fieldset> für gruppierte Controls

3. Color-Contrast:
   → Alle Text-Farben-Kombinationen:
     - Normal text: Min. 4.5:1
     - Large text (18pt+): Min. 3:1
     - UI-Components: Min. 3:1
   → Validierung via contrast-checker beim Theme-Wechsel
   → High-Contrast-Theme als Fallback

4. Reduced-Motion:
   → Respektiere prefers-reduced-motion:
     @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
       --bdg-transition-fast: 0ms
       --bdg-transition-base: 0ms
       --bdg-transition-slow: 0ms
     }
   → Animationen müssen deaktivierbar sein
   → State-Change ohne Animation muss funktional identisch sein
```

### 3.3 Testing-Strategie

**Multi-Layer Test-Architektur:**

```
Layer 1: Unit-Tests (80% Coverage-Ziel)
  → Teste jede Klassen-Methode isoliert
  → Mocke Dependencies (StateManager, DOMController)
  → Fokus auf:
    - Mathematische Logik (Snap-Algorithmus)
    - Validation-Funktionen
    - State-Transitions
  → Framework: Jest oder Vitest
  → Test-Dateien: *.spec.js neben Source-Files

Layer 2: Integration-Tests (60% Coverage-Ziel)
  → Teste Interaktion zwischen Modulen
  → Beispiele:
    - ImageProcessor → StateManager → CanvasRenderer
    - DOMController → ThemeEngine → CSS-Sync
  → Nutze echte DOM (JSDOM oder happy-dom)
  → Teste async Flows Ende-zu-Ende

Layer 3: E2E-Tests (Critical User Journeys)
  → Framework: Playwright oder Cypress
  → Test-Scenarios:
    1. Complete Badge Creation Flow:
       - Upload Bild
       - Setze Name/Title
       - Adjustiere Rotation/Scale
       - Export als PNG
       - Validiere Download
    
    2. Theme-Switching:
       - Wechsle von Light zu Dark
       - Prüfe alle CSS-Variablen
       - Validiere Kontrast-Ratios
    
    3. Error-Recovery:
       - Upload invalide Datei
       - Prüfe Error-Message
       - Upload valide Datei
       - Prüfe Success-State
  
  → Visual-Regression-Tests:
    - Snapshot-Vergleich von Canvas-Outputs
    - Tolerate 0.1% Pixel-Differenz (Anti-Aliasing)

Layer 4: Performance-Tests
  → Metrics:
    - First Contentful Paint < 1.5s
    - Time to Interactive < 3.0s
    - Canvas-Render-Time < 16ms (60fps)
    - Export-Zeit (scale=4) < 5s
  
  → Tools:
    - Lighthouse CI im Build-Pipeline
    - Custom Performance-Marks:
      performance.mark('render-start')
      performance.mark('render-end')
      performance.measure('render-duration', 'render-start', 'render-end')
  
  → Load-Tests:
    - 100 schnelle State-Changes hintereinander
    - 10 simultane Exports (sollte queuen, nicht crashen)

Layer 5: Accessibility-Tests
  → Automatisiert:
    - axe-core via @axe-core/playwright
    - Prüfe WCAG-Violations bei jedem Build
  
  → Manuell:
    - Screen-Reader-Test-Checklist (NVDA, JAWS, VoiceOver)
    - Keyboard-only Navigation-Test
    - High-Contrast-Mode-Test
```

---

## 4. Deployment & Build-Spezifikation

**Build-Pipeline-Architektur:**

```
Stage 1: Development-Build
  → Bundler: Vite oder Webpack
  → Output:
    - Unminified JavaScript mit Source-Maps
    - Separate CSS-Datei (nicht inlined)
    - Hot-Module-Replacement aktiv
  → Development-Server:
    - Port 3000
    - CORS permissive für lokales Testing
  → Environment-Variables:
    - DEBUG_MODE=true
    - LOG_LEVEL=verbose

Stage 2: Staging-Build
  → Code-Splitting:
    - Main-Bundle: App-Core (< 100KB)
    - Vendor-Bundle: External-Libraries (< 200KB)
    - Async-Chunks: Export-Module, Theme-Engine (lazy-loaded)
  → Optimizations:
    - Tree-Shaking für unused exports
    - Minification (Terser für JS, cssnano für CSS)
    - Image-Optimization (Sharp für Asset-Pipeline)
  → Testing:
    - Alle Unit/Integration-Tests müssen grün sein
    - E2E-Tests gegen Staging-URL
  → Environment:
    - DEBUG_MODE=false
    - ERROR_REPORTING=staging-service-url

Stage 3: Production-Build
  → Zusätzliche Optimizations:
    - Brotli-Compression (Level 11)
    - Resource-Hints:
      <link rel="preload" as="script" href="main.js">
      <link rel="prefetch" href="export-module.js">
    - Service-Worker für Offline-Capability
  → Security-Headers:
    - Content-Security-Policy
    - X-Frame-Options: DENY
    - Permissions-Policy
  → CDN-Deployment:
    - Static-Assets zu CDN (Cloudflare/CloudFront)
    - Cache-Control: max-age=31536000 für versionierte Assets
    - Cache-Busting via File-Hash in Filename
  → Monitoring:
    - Error-Tracking (Sentry)
    - Performance-Monitoring (Web-Vitals zu Analytics)
    - Usage-Analytics (Privacy-friendly)

Rollback-Strategie:
  → Blue-Green-Deployment:
    - Neue Version läuft parallel zu alter
    - Traffic-Switch via Load-Balancer
    - Bei Fehler: Instant-Rollback zu Blau
  → Canary-Releases:
    - 5% User sehen neue Version
    - Monitor Error-Rate
    - Bei Stabilität: Graduell auf 100%
```

---

## 5. Dokumentations-Struktur

**Vier-Säulen-Dokumentation:**

```
Säule 1: Architektur-Dokumentation (dieses Dokument)
  → Für: Software-Architekten, Senior-Developers
  → Inhalt:
    - Klassendiagramme
    - Sequence-Diagramme
    - State-Machine-Definitionen
    - Traceability-Matrizen
  → Format: Markdown mit Mermaid-Diagrammen
  → Versionierung: Git-tracked, Major-Version bei Breaking-Changes

Säule 2: API-Dokumentation
  → Für: Developers, Plugin-Entwickler
  → Inhalt:
    - Alle öffentlichen Methoden mit JSDoc
    - TypeScript-Interfaces (auch wenn kein TS verwendet)
    - Beispiel-Nutzung für jede Methode
    - Deprecation-Notices
  → Format: Generiert via TypeDoc oder JSDoc
  → Hosting: Static-Site auf docs.subdomain.com

Säule 3: User-Guide
  → Für: End-User, Content-Creator
  → Inhalt:
    - Step-by-Step-Tutorials
    - Video-Walkthroughs (3-5 Min)
    - FAQ-Sektion
    - Troubleshooting-Guide
  → Format: Interactive-Web-App (selbst Badge-Generator nutzen für Demos)
  → Sprachen: Deutsch, Englisch

Säule 4: Onboarding-Guide
  → Für: Neue Entwickler im Team
  → Inhalt:
    - Setup-Anleitung (< 30 Min bis lauffähig)
    - Architektur-Walkthrough
    - Contribution-Guidelines
    - Code-Style-Guide
  → Format: Markdown mit verlinkten Code-Beispielen
  → Location: /docs/ONBOARDING.md im Repo
```

## 6. Zusammenfassung & Nächste Schritte

### Vollständigkeit der Spezifikation

Diese Architektur-Spezifikation definiert:

✅ **Strukturelle Ebene:**
- Komplette Klassenhierarchie mit 6 Haupt-Modulen
- 100+ definierte Methoden-Signaturen (ohne Code)
- Vollständiges Naming-Schema für alle DOM-Hooks
- 50+ CSS-Variablen für Theme-System

✅ **Logische Ebene:**
- 3 detaillierte Sequence-Diagramme
- Traceability-Matrix mit 12+ UI-Flows
- 3 Algorithmus-Beschreibungen (Snap, Error-Handling, Export)
- State-Machine-Definitionen

✅ **Qualitäts-Ebene:**
- 3 identifizierte Schwachstellen + Mitigations
- 5-Layer-Testing-Strategie
- WCAG 2.1 AA Compliance-Spec
- Performance-Budgets definiert

### Implementierungs-Roadmap

**Phase 1 (Woche 1-2): Foundation**
- StateManager implementieren
- DOMController mit Validation-Layer
- Theme-Engine mit CSS-Variable-Sync
- Unit-Tests für Core-Logic

**Phase 2 (Woche 3-4): Rendering & Processing**
- CanvasRenderer mit Render-Queue
- ImageProcessor mit async Upload
- Integration-Tests für Render-Pipeline
- Performance-Profiling

**Phase 3 (Woche 5-6): Export & Plugins**
- ExportModule mit multi-format Support
- Plugin-System-Interface
- E2E-Tests für User-Journeys
- Accessibility-Audit

**Phase 4 (Woche 7-8): Polish & Deploy**
- Error-Recovery-Strategien implementieren
- Production-Build-Pipeline
- Dokumentation finalisieren
- Beta-Launch mit Monitoring

### Offene Architektur-Fragen

1. **Internationalisierung:**
   - Soll i18n von Anfang an eingebaut werden?
   - Format: JSON-Dateien oder ICU-Message-Format?

2. **Persistenz:**
   - LocalStorage für Auto-Save?
   - Cloud-Sync für Multi-Device?

3. **Kollaboration:**
   - Soll Real-Time-Kollaboration möglich sein?
   - WebRTC oder WebSocket-basiert?

---

**Architektur-Status: ✅ VOLLSTÄNDIG SPEZIFIZIERT**

*Dieses Dokument ist die Single-Source-of-Truth für alle Implementierungs-Entscheidungen. Änderungen erfordern Architecture-Review-Meeting.*

# Contributing to DIN 5008 Generator

> **Role:** Human Developer Guide.
> **Philosophy:** Brain-First, Code-Second.

## 🛑 STOP! Read Before Coding

This project uses a strict **Traceability Architecture**.
You cannot just "edit the code".

### The Workflow

1.  **Check the Brain**: Look into `.brain/` folder.
    - Is the feature defined in `02_business_logic.md`?
    - Is the UI defined in `04_ui_design.md`?
2.  **Update the Brain**:
    - If you add a feature, add a Spec-ID (e.g., `[DIN-FEAT-NEW]`) to the brain first.
    - Update `06_roadmap_todo.md`.
3.  **Code**:
    - Implement changes.
    - **MUST**: Add the Spec-ID as a comment near the code block.
    - Example: `// [DIN-FEAT-NEW] Implemented logic here`
4.  **Verify**:
    - Run `App.Tests.run()` in the browser console (or via UI).

### Project Structure

- **.brain/**: The Boss. Source of Truth.
- **js/core/**: State & Config.
- **js/logic/**: Pure functions (easier to test).
- **js/ui/**: DOM manipulation (harder to test).

### Testing

Run the in-browser test suite via the "System Diagnostics" dialog or strictly in console:
```javascript
await window.App.Tests.run();
```

<!--
 > File: index.html
 > Role: Application Entry Point & DOM Root
 > Spec: [DIN.UI.SHELL.BIND]
 > Dep:  css/theme.css, js/core/app.js, sw.js
-->
<!DOCTYPE html>

    <!-- [DIN.PERF.FOUC] Critical State Restoration (Anti-Flicker) -->
    <script>
      (function() {
        try {
          // PLATINUM: Check for new key first
          let raw = localStorage.getItem('platinum_brief_state');
          let state = null;
          
          if (raw) {
            const packet = JSON.parse(raw);
            // Check if it's the new envelope (has .data) or legacy raw
            state = packet.data ? packet.data : packet;
          } else {
            // Fallback to legacy key
            raw = localStorage.getItem('antigravity_din_state');
            if (raw) state = JSON.parse(raw);
          }

          if (state && state.config) {
            const cfg = state.config;
            // 1. Layout
            if (cfg.layout === 'form-a') {
              document.body.classList.remove('layout-form-b');
              document.body.classList.add('layout-form-a');
            } else {
              document.body.classList.remove('layout-form-a');
              document.body.classList.add('layout-form-b');
            }

            // 2. Font (Aptos vs Serif)
            if (cfg.font === 'serif') {
               document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--font-stack', "'Times New Roman', 'Garamond', serif");
            } else {
               document.documentElement.style.setProperty('--font-stack', "'Aptos', 'Segoe UI', 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif");
            }
          }
        } catch(e) { console.warn('FOUC Script Error', e); }
      })();
    </script>
    <!-- [DIN.SYS.STABL.LOCK] Mobile Blocker Overlay -->

--- START DATEI: LLM_OPTIMIZATION.md | Format: .md | Größe: 1,67 KB | Zeichen: 1709 [TEXT] ---
# 🤖 LLM Implementation Guide (Platinum Standard)

**ROLE:** You are the Senior Architect of this project.
**MISSION:** Maintain the `DIN 5008 Brief Generator` as a Zero-Dependency, Offline-First Vanilla JS artifact.

## 1. The Prime Directives

1. **NO BUILD TOOLS**: Do not suggest `npm install`, `vite`, `webpack`, or `typescript`. This project runs on `file://`.
2. **NO EXTERNAL LIBS**: Do not import React, Vue, jQuery, or Google Fonts via CDN. Everything must be local.
3. **THE CORTEX IS KING**: The `.brain/` directory is the Source of Truth.
    * Start every session by reading `.brain/00_project_memory.md`.
    * If code contradicts documentation, update the code.

## 2. File Structure Strategy

- `index.html`: The only entry point. Links relative `./css` and `./js`.
* `js/core/`: Singleton services (`App`, `Logger`).
* `js/ui/`: UI Manipulation Logic.
* **DO NOT CREATE**: `.jsx`, `.tsx`, `.scss` files.

## 3. How to make changes (The "Surgical" Method)

When the user asks for a feature:

1. **Check Spec-ID**: Does it exist in `.brain/02_business_logic.md`? If not, create it there first.
2. **Edit CSS**: Add vanilla CSS variables in `css/theme.css` if needed.
3. **Edit JS**: Use ES6 Modules.

    ```javascript
    // ✅ GOOD
    export const myFeature = () => { ... }
    
    // ❌ BAD
    import React from 'react';
    require('lib');
    ```

## 4. Troubleshooting

- **"It works on my machine but not yours"**: Check for absolute paths (`/css/...` vs `./css/...`). Always use relative `./`.
* **"The styling looks broken"**: Check `index.html` for missing `<link>` tags.

---
*This file is for AI Agents. Read it to align your weights.*

--- START DATEI: README.md | Format: .md | Größe: 1,46 KB | Zeichen: 1471 [TEXT] ---
# DIN 5008 Brief-Generator (Platinum Standard)

> **File:** `README.md`
> **Role:** Entry Point & Project Manifest.
> **Dependencies:** Links to `.brain/` for logic.
> **Description:** High-level overview and setup instructions.

Ein professioneller, rein clientseitiger (Vanilla JS) Editor für Briefe nach DIN 5008.

> **⚠️ ARCHITECTURE WARNING**
> Dieses Projekt folgt der **Brain-First Architecture**.
> Der Quellcode (`js/`, `css/`, `html`) ist lediglich ein Artefakt der Dokumentation.
>
> **Änderungen dürfen NIEMALS direkt im Code beginnen.**
> 1. Erst `.brain/` aktualisieren (Logik, Specs, Roadmap).
> 2. Dann Code schreiben.
> 3. Traceability-ID (`[DIN-...]`) im Code referenzieren.

## 📂 Struktur

- **.brain/**: Die "Single Source of Truth". Alle Regeln, IDs und Pläne.
- **css/**:
  - `theme.css`: Farben, Variablen, Dark/Light Mode.
  - `din5008-paper.css`: Die physikalische Papier-Definition (mm-genau).
  - `app-ui.css`: UI-Komponenten (Sidebar, Dialoge).
- **js/**:
  - `core/`: State Management & Orchestrierung.
  - `logic/`: Reine Funktionen (Formatter, Utils, Logger).
  - `services/`: I/O (API, LocalStorage).
  - `ui/`: DOM-Manipulation (keine Business-Logik hier!).

## 🚀 Nutzung

Einfach `index.html` im Browser öffnen. Keine Installation notwendig.

## 🛠️ Tools

Um das Projekt in eine einzelne HTML-Datei zu bündeln (für E-Mail-Versand oder Archivierung):

```bash
node tools/bundler.js
```

Ausgabe liegt dann in `dist/index_bundled.html`.
--- ENDE  README.md ---

--- START DATEI: sw.js | Format: .js | Größe: 2,05 KB | Zeichen: 2099 [TEXT] ---
/**
 * > File: sw.js
 * > Role: Service Worker (PWA Core)
 * > Spec: [DIN.SYS.PWA.CACHE]
 * > Dep:  (Standalone)
 */

const CACHE_NAME = 'din5008-v9-platinum';
const ASSETS = [
    './index.html',
    './manifest.json',
    './css/theme.css',
    './css/din5008-paper.css',
    './css/app-ui.css',
    './js/core/config.js',
    './js/core/constants.js',
    './js/core/state.js',
    './js/core/app.js',
    './js/core/utils.js',
    './js/core/logger.js',
    './js/formatters/date-formatter.js',
    './js/formatters/address-parser.js',
    './js/formatters/salutation-engine.js',
    './js/formatters/formatter.js',
    './js/services/api.js',
    './js/services/storage.js',
    './js/ui/ui-manager.js',
    './js/modules/diagnostics.js',
    './js/modules/tests.js',
    './js/controllers/settings-controller.js',
    './js/controllers/document-controller.js'
];

self.addEventListener('install', (event) => {
    event.waitUntil(
        caches.open(CACHE_NAME)
            .then((cache) => cache.addAll(ASSETS))
    );
});

self.addEventListener('fetch', (event) => {
    event.respondWith(
        caches.match(event.request).then((response) => {
            return response || fetch(event.request).then(netResponse => {
                if (!netResponse || netResponse.status !== 200 || netResponse.type !== 'basic') {
                    return netResponse;
                }
                const responseToCache = netResponse.clone();
                caches.open(CACHE_NAME).then((cache) => {
                    cache.put(event.request, responseToCache);
                });
                return netResponse;
            });
        })
    );
});

self.addEventListener('activate', (event) => {
    const cacheWhitelist = [CACHE_NAME];
    event.waitUntil(
        caches.keys().then((cacheNames) => {
            return Promise.all(
                cacheNames.map((cacheName) => {
                    if (cacheWhitelist.indexOf(cacheName) === -1) {
                        return caches.delete(cacheName);
                    }
                })
            );
        })
    );
});
--- ENDE  sw.js ---

<!--
> File: .brain/00_project_memory.md
> Role: Project Context & Meta-Memory
> Spec: DIN.BRAIN.MEMORY
> Last-Update: 2026-01-22
> Dependencies: All .brain/ files, README.md
> Description: The "Single Source of Truth" for project health.
-->

# 📜 V9 PLATINUM CONSTITUTION (The 10 Commandments)
> **Spec-ID:** [DIN.SYS.LAW]
> **Status:** IMMUTABLE

1.  **TRUTH:** The `.brain/` folder is the Single Source of Truth. If code contradicts the Brain, the Code is wrong.
2.  **HYBRID ARCHITECTURE (THE GOLDEN RULE):** The Data Schema (`storage.js`) contains MORE fields than the UI (`index.html`). This is INTENTIONAL. NEVER add visible inputs for fields like `signature_role` or `footer_col2` just because they exist in the data.
3.  **VISUAL FREEZE:** The CSS layout (`css/din5008-paper.css`) is IMMUTABLE. Zero-Pixel-Shift. No new borders, no new margins.
4.  **BACKEND TRUTH:** `.brain/02_business_logic.xml` must list the FULL Schema (including hidden fields) so the system knows they exist, but the UI must remain MINIMALIST.
5.  **NO GHOST DATA:** If a field is in Schema but not UI, the Storage Logic must PRESERVE its value ("Pass-Through") during save/load.
6.  **USER SOVEREIGNTY:** Automation NEVER overwrites manual user input. `isDirty` flags protect user content.
7.  **NAMING PARITY:** Terminology in code must match documentation exactly.
8.  **CLEANLINESS:** No dead code. No zombie CSS. No `console.log` in production (Use `App.Logger`).
9.  **SAFETY:** Secrets (API Keys) are for execution only. MASK them (`***`) before exporting JSON.
10. **PURITY:** Vanilla JS Only. No Frameworks. No Polyfills. No Build Steps.

---

### 🛑 TECH RADAR: STRICT BANS [DIN-OPS-BAN]
This project follows a strict "Vanilla First" philosophy to maintain LLM-friendliness and long-term maintainability.

| Technology | Status | Reason | Replacement |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Frameworks** (React, Vue, Angular) | **BANNED** ⛔ | Increases complexity, hides DOM logic, adds build steps. | Native DOM API, ES6 Modules. |
| **Pixel-PDF** (jspdf, html2canvas) | **BANNED** ⛔ | blurry text, non-searchable PDFs, heavy bundles. | Native `window.print()` (Browser PDF Engine). |
| **TypeScript** (Runtime) | **AVOID** ⚠️ | Build step required. JSDoc is preferred for types. | JSDoc Comments (`/** ... */`). |
| **jQuery / Lodash** | **BANNED** ⛔ | Redundant in modern JS. | Native `fetch`, `querySelector`, `Array.map`. |

> **Directive:** Any AI suggestion involving banned technologies must be rejected immediately.

## 1. Global Documentation Protocol (META-RULES)
> **RULE 1: NO DELETION.** Content in ANY `.md` file in this project must NEVER be deleted.
> **RULE 2: DEPRECATION.** Move obsolete rules to `### Deprecated / Legacy Decisions ###`.

## 2. Active Architectural Decision Records (ADRs)

### ADR-011: Persistent Location Bias [DIN-FEAT-RADIUS-HYBRID]
- **Status**: Accepted
- **Decision**: Cascade: Profile > Geocoding > GPS (One-time save).

### ADR-015: Split-Screen App Shell [DIN-LAYOUT-SPLIT]
- **Status**: Accepted (V9)
- **Context**: Users want "Word-like" experience.
- **Decision**: strict "App Shell" architecture. 
    - Body: `height: 100vh`, `overflow: hidden`.
    - Sidebar: Fixed 300px left, internal scrolling (Sandwich Layout).
    - Workspace: Fluid right, internal scrolling (A4 Paper lives here).
- **Consequences**: Zero Layout Shift on scroll.

### ADR-016: PWA & Offline-First [DIN-PWA]
- **Status**: Accepted (V9)
- **Decision**: Implement `sw.js` with Cache-First strategy.
- **Reason**: App must work 100% without internet (except API calls).
- **Assets**: `manifest.json` and offline fonts prepared.

### ADR-017: Direct Settings Control [DIN-FEAT-DIRECT-SETTINGS]
- **Status**: Accepted (V9)
- **Decision**: Move System/API settings from Modal to Inline Sidebar Controls.
- **Reason**: Reduce clicks. Instant feedback on layout/formality changes.

### ADR-018: Legacy Feature Restoration [DIN-UX-LEGACY]
- **Status**: Accepted
- **Decision**: Re-implement "History Stack" (Undo/Redo), "Smart Filenames" (Export), and "Toast Notifications".

### ADR-019: Desktop Stabilization Focus [DIN-SYS-STABLE]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED (CRITICAL)**
- **Date**: 2026-01-12
- **Context**: System instability due to responsive complexity.
- **Decision**: **STRICT DESKTOP ONLY**. Mobile layouts, drawers, and hamburger menus are DISABLED.
- **Reason**: Prioritize core functionality and print reliability over mobile usage.
- **Consequence**: Screens < 1024px show a blocking warning message.

### ADR-020: Native Print Strategy (No JS-PDF) [DIN-FEAT-PDF-NATIVE]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED**
- **Date**: 2026-01-12
- **Update**: 2026-01-12 (Hardened)
- **Context**: User requested robust PDF export. Evaluated `window.print()` vs `jspdf`.
- **Decision**: Rely 100% on **Native Browser Print** (`window.print()`).
- **Forbidden**: `jspdf`, `html2canvas` are BANNED.
- **Reasoning**: 
    1. **Text Selectability (Killer Criterion)**: JS Libraries often rasterize text to images, breaking Search/Copy-Paste.
    2. **Security**: Browsers prohibit silent PDF generation.
    3. **Precision**: DIN 5008 requires exact mm margins. CSS `@media print` handles this natively.
    4. **Performance**: Avoids ~1MB bundle size increase.

### ADR-021: Machine-Readable Roadmap [DIN-OPS-XML]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED**
- **Date**: 2026-01-12
- **Context**: Markdown Todo lists are hard for automated tools to parse.
- **Decision**: The Roadmap is now strictly managed in `.brain/06_roadmap.xml`.
- **Legacy**: `.brain/06_roadmap_todo.md` is deprecated but preserved for history.

### ADR-022: Context-Aware UI Patterns [DIN-UX-CONTEXT]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED**
- **Date**: 2026-01-12
- **Context**: API Key was buried in Profile Dialog, causing UX friction when switching providers. Status bar was distracting in Sidebar header.
- **Decision**: 
    1. **API Key**: Moves to Sidebar (Zone 2). Hidden by default, appears only when "Geoapify" is selected.
    2. **Modal UX**: Modals must be dismissible via "X" button AND Backdrop click.
    3. **Status Bar**: Moves to Bottom-Right Overlay (unobtrusive).

### ADR-023: Clean Sidebar Policy [DIN-UX-CLEAN]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED**
- **Date**: 2026-01-12
- **Decision**: The Sidebar is exclusively for "Active Document Controls" (Settings, Print). "Passive Data" (History, Saved Profiles) moves to separate Modals/Overlays to prevent visual clutter.
- **Reason**: Prevent clutter. Enable "Flat UX".
- **Consequence**: 
    1. History List removed from Sidebar. 
    2. Storage logic REMAINS active in background. 
    3. Future UI will be a Fullscreen Overlay ("Time Machine").

### ADR-024: Zero-Layout-Shift Visual Guides [DIN.UI.VISUAL.GUIDES]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED**
- **Date**: 2026-01-14
- **Decision**: Use CSS `outline` and `outline-offset` instead of `border` for visual guides (Hilfslinien).
- **Reason**: Borders add width/height to the Box Model, causing reflows and potentially pushing content to a new page (Multipage Error). Outlines are purely visual and do not affect geometry.

### ADR-025: Conditional API Heartbeat (Ghost Mode) [DIN.SYS.API.HEARTBEAT]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED**
- **Date**: 2026-01-14
- **Decision**: 
    1. **Storage**: API Key is stored, but UI Input is hidden (`.key-set`) if valid.
    2. **Validation**: Check runs on Init, on Provider Change, and every 60 mins.
    3. **Efficiency**: Validation ONLY runs if Provider == Geoapify.
    4. **Recovery**: If validation fails, Input reappears automatically.

### ADR-026: Guardrail Fields Strategy [DIN.UI.INPUT.GUARD]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED**
- **Date**: 2026-01-14
- **Context**: Free-text address fields led to bad formatting.
- **Decision**: Split Recipient Address into 4 rigid fields: Company/Extra, Name, Street, ZIP+City.
- **Rules**:
    - **Smart Split**: ZIP and City have separate fields but share a Flex-Row.
    - **Trigger**: Autocomplete for ZIP needs 3 chars; City needs 2 chars.
    - **Bias**: Hardcoded fallback to "Bonn" (50.7333, 7.0950) if no User Profile exists.

### ADR-027: Automation "Dirty Flag" [DIN.LOGIC.AUTO.GUARD]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED**
- **Date**: 2026-01-14
- **Decision**: If a user manually edits an auto-generated field (e.g., Salutation), set a `isDirty` flag.
- **Effect**: Subsequent auto-generation logic (e.g., changing Recipient Name) MUST respect this flag and NOT overwrite the user's manual edit.

### ADR-028: Platinum Logging Framework [DIN.SYS.LOG.STYLE]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED**
- **Date**: 2026-01-14
- **Decision**: Implement `App.Logger` with CSS-styled console output (`%c`) and Decision Tracing.
- **Channels**: `info` (Blue), `success` (Green), `warn` (Yellow), `error` (Red).
- **Tracing**: Log *why* logic decisions were made (e.g., "Gender: m detected -> Formal").

### ADR-029: 5-Layer Class Architecture [DIN-ARCH-V9]
- **Status**: **ACCEPTED (CRITICAL)**
- **Date**: 2026-01-22
- **Context**: Codebase became spaghetti-like with Namespace Pattern. ID-based logic was brittle.
- **Decision**: strict 5-Layer Architecture:
    1. **StateManager**: Central Truth (Proxy).
    2. **FormatterService**: Pure Logic.
    3. **DOMController**: Logic-less UI Bridge.
    4. **ExportModule**: IO.
    5. **BriefApp**: Orchestrator.
- **Traceability**: logic relies exclusively on `data-js` and `data-sync`. IDs are CSS-only.

### Deprecated / Legacy Decisions

#### ADR-000: Namespace Object Pattern (window.App)
- **Status**: **DEPRECATED**
- **Reason**: Global namespace pollution and tight coupling. Replaced by Class Instantiation.

#### ADR-000: Logic-via-ID
- **Status**: **BANNED**
- **Reason**: Breaks when UI changes. Replaced by `data-js` attributes.

#### ADR-013: Mobile UI Pro-Refinement [DIN-UI-MOBILE-V3]
- **Status**: **SUSPENDED** (Replaced by ADR-019).
- **Reason**: Caused layout jitter and instability. Mobile support is paused until Desktop V9 is perfect.

## 3. PARKED CONCEPTS / REDESIGNS

### [DIN.UX.HISTORY.V2] The "Time Machine"
- **Status**: CONCEPT PHASE (High Priority)
- **Vision**: "Flat UX" solution for Drafts/History.
- **Mechanism**: Dedicated Icon -> Fullscreen Overlay / Slide-Over Drawer.
- **Goal**: Large previews (Tiles) instead of text lists. 
- **Constraint**: `StorageService` must continue saving data in the background, even if currently invisible.

## 4. Implementation Status
- **Current Version**: V9 Platinum (2026-01-22 Architecture).
- **Focus**: Migration to 5-Layer Class System.
- **Roadmap**: Managed in `.brain/06_roadmap.xml`.
--- ENDE  .brain\00_project_memory.md ---

--- START DATEI: .brain\01_system_instructions.xml | Format: .xml | Größe: 2,11 KB | Zeichen: 2158 [TEXT] ---
<!--
> File: .brain/01_system_instructions.xml
> Role: System Core Instructions
> Spec: DIN.BRAIN.SYS
> Last-Update: 2026-01-22
-->
<system_instructions>
    <meta>
        <role>Senior Architect & Guardian of the Platinum Standard</role>
        <project>DIN 5008 Brief Generator (V9 Platinum)</project>
        <iteration>34_ISO</iteration>
        <philosophy>Notebooklm.md</philosophy>
    </meta>

    <prime_directives>
        <directive id="1" title="PLATINUM ARCHITECTURE (5-LAYER)">
            <rule> STRICT Class Separation: StateManager, FormatterService, DOMController, ExportModule, BriefApp.</rule>
            <rule> NO Global Namespace Pollution (window.App.* is DEPRECATED).</rule>
            <rule> Pure Vanilla JS (ES6 Classes) only.</rule>
        </directive>

        <directive id="2" title="TRACEABILITY & BINDING">
            <rule> NO Logic-via-ID. Logic MUST use `data-js` attributes.</rule>
            <rule> NO `document.getElementById` for event binding. Use `DOMController` delegation.</rule>
            <rule> Bi-directional Sync: `data-sync="state:[path]"` is mandatory for inputs.</rule>
        </directive>

        <directive id="3" title="STATE SOVEREIGNTY">
            <rule> StateManager is the Single Source of Truth.</rule>
            <rule> Never mutate state directly. Use `stateManager.state.prop = val` via Proxy.</rule>
            <rule> DOM Updates must be Reactive (Subscriber Pattern).</rule>
        </directive>

        <directive id="4" title="SCHEMA SUPREMACY">
            <rule>The JSON Schema (`js/services/storage.js`) is the Master.</rule>
            <rule>Do not invent new fields if the Schema already covers it.</rule>
        </directive>
    </prime_directives>

    <workflow_protocol>
        <step>1. CHECK `architecture_map.md` & `isomorphism_registry.xml`</step>
        <step>2. DEFINE Spec-IDs in `.brain`</step>
        <step>3. IMPLEMENT Logic in `FormatterService` (Pure Function)</step>
        <step>4. BIND via `DOMController` (data-js/data-sync)</step>
        <step>5. VERIFY State Cycles (Input -> Proxy -> DOM)</step>
    </workflow_protocol>

</system_instructions>

  <!-- 2. DETAILED SPECIFICATIONS (V9 PLATINUM) -->
  <specifications>

    <!-- [DIN.LOGIC.SALUTATION] THE MATRIX -->
    <logic_matrix id="DIN.LOGIC.SALUTATION">
      <style name="FORMAL">
        <case recipient="Known (M)">"Sehr geehrter Herr [Name],"</case>
        <case recipient="Known (F)">"Sehr geehrte Frau [Name],"</case>
        <case recipient="Unknown">"Sehr geehrte Damen und Herren,"</case>
        <greeting>"Mit freundlichen Grüßen"</greeting>
      </style>
      <style name="POLITE">
        <case recipient="Known (M)">"Guten Tag, Herr [Name],"</case>
        <case recipient="Known (F)">"Guten Tag, Frau [Name],"</case>
        <case recipient="Unknown">"Guten Tag,"</case>
        <greeting>"Freundliche Grüße"</greeting>
      </style>
      <style name="CASUAL">
        <case recipient="Known">"Hallo [Vorname],"</case>
        <case recipient="Unknown">"Hallo zusammen,"</case>
        <greeting>"Viele Grüße"</greeting>
      </style>
    </logic_matrix>

    <spec id="DIN.FEAT.GEO.BIAS">
      <description>
            Smart Autocomplete with Location Bias.
      </description>
      <requirements>
        <req>Hardcoded Fallback: Bonn (50.7333, 7.0950) if no Profile coords.</req>
        <req>Convert User Profile Address -> Lat/Lon (Geocoding).</req>
        <req>Cache Lat/Lon in Profile to avoid re-fetching.</req>
        <req>Inject '&amp;bias=proximity:lon,lat' into Autocomplete API calls.</req>
      </requirements>
    </spec>

    <spec id="DIN.LOGIC.AUTO.GUARD">
      <description>
            Protects manual user edits from being overwritten by automation (Salutation).
      </description>
      <requirements>
        <req>Controller tracks 'isSalutationDirty' boolean.</req>
        <req>Set to TRUE on manual 'input' in Salutation field.</req>
        <req>Reset to FALSE only on 'New/Reset' action.</req>
        <req>Automation (parseRecipient) aborts if Dirty is TRUE.</req>
      </requirements>
    </spec>

    <spec id="DIN.SYS.API.HEARTBEAT">
      <description>
            Lifecyle Management for API Keys.
      </description>
      <requirements>
        <req>On Init: Validate Key.</req>
        <req>On Provider Switch (to Geoapify): Validate immediately.</req>
        <req>Loop: Check every 60 mins ONLY if Provider == Geoapify.</req>
        <req>UI: Hide input if valid (Ghost Mode). Show if invalid.</req>
      </requirements>
    </spec>

    <spec id="DIN.UI.SIDEBAR">
      <description>
            Fixed sidebar (left). Contains ONLY active document controls.
            Passive data (History, Profiles) moved to Modals/Overlays for "Clean UX".
      </description>
      <requirements>
        <req>Must accommodate "Sandwich Layout" (Header, Scroll-Area, Fixed Footer).</req>
        <req>Must hide native scrollbars unless necessary.</req>
        <req>Reset Key link must be discreetly placed in footer.</req>
      </requirements>
    </spec>

    <spec id="DIN.UI.INPUT.GUARD">
      <description>
            Structured Recipient Block (The Guardrails).
      </description>
      <requirements>
        <req>4 Fixed Lines: Company, Name, Street, ZIP/City.</req>
        <req>ZIP and City share a flex-row but are distinct inputs.</req>
        <req>ZIP Trigger: Autocomplete starts after 3 chars.</req>
        <req>City Trigger: Autocomplete starts after 2 chars.</req>
      </requirements>
    </spec>

    <spec id="DIN.LOGIC.SMART.FILL">
      <description>
            Cross-Filling logic for Address fields.
      </description>
      <requirements>
        <req>Selecting a suggestion in ZIP input auto-fills City.</req>
        <req>Selecting a suggestion in City input auto-fills ZIP.</req>
      </requirements>
    </spec>

    <spec id="DIN.LOGIC.OVERFLOW">
      <description>
            Prevents content from pushing the signature off the physical A4 page.
      </description>
      <requirements>
        <req>Monitor distance between Signature-Bottom and Page-Bottom.</req>
        <req>IF distance &lt; 20mm: Block new input (keydown).</req>
        <req>Show visual Toast warning ("Seite voll").</req>
      </requirements>
    </spec>

    <spec id="DIN.LOGIC.DATE.FMT">
      <description>
            Logic anchor for the date field formatting.
      </description>
      <requirements>
        <req>WYSIWYG: The trigger button must be overlay/absolute to prevent text shift when printing.</req>
      </requirements>
    </spec>

    <spec id="DIN.SYS.LOG.STYLE">
      <description>
            Platinum Logging Framework.
      </description>
      <requirements>
        <req>Use CSS styling (%c) in Console.</req>
        <req>Categories: Info (Blue), Success (Green), Warn (Yellow), Error (Red).</req>
        <req>Must log "Decision Logic" (e.g. Salutation derivation reason).</req>
      </requirements>
    </spec>

  </specifications>

  <!-- 2. LOGIC RULES -->
  <logic_rules>

    <!-- Reactive Proxy Cycle -->
    <rule_group id="DIN.SYS.REACTIVE" title="State Management Cycle">
      <rule type="flow">Input (User) -> DOMController (Event Listener) -> StateManager (Proxy Set)</rule>
      <rule type="flow">State Change (Proxy) -> DOMController (Subscriber) -> DOM Update (View)</rule>
      <rule type="constraint">Views MUST NOT update State directly. They must go through the Controller.</rule>
    </rule_group>

    <!-- Desktop Mandate -->
    <rule_group id="DIN.SYS.STABLE" title="Desktop Mandate">
      <rule type="constraint">Application strictly designed for Desktop Browsers (> 1024px width).</rule>
    </rule_group>

    <!-- Address Validation -->
    <rule_group id="DIN.LOGIC.VALIDATION" title="Address Validation">
      <validation_check type="zip">Must contain 4 or 5 consecutive digits.</validation_check>
      <ui_feedback severity="warning">Visual warning only.</ui_feedback>
    </rule_group>

  </logic_rules>

</business_logic>

--- START DATEI: .brain\02_isomorphism_registry.xml | Format: .xml | Größe: 7,75 KB | Zeichen: 7932 [TEXT] ---
<!--
> File: .brain/02_isomorphism_registry.xml

<isomorphism_registry>
    <meta>
        <purpose>Traceability Matrix: Linking Logic (JSON) to Visuals (UI) and Code.</purpose>
        <status>Active</status>
        <version>2.0 (Omni-Complete)</version>
    </meta>

    <isomorphism_rules>
        <rule>
            <type>Naming Convention</type>
            <pattern>data-js="[type]:[name]"</pattern>
            <example>data-js="field:sender-name"</example>
            <types>
                <type id="field">Input/Output Element (Bi-directional)</type>
                <type id="action">Event Trigger (Button/Click)</type>
                <type id="view">Read-Only Display (Toast/Overlay)</type>
            </types>
        </rule>
        <rule>
            <type>State Synchronization</type>
            <pattern>data-sync="state:[path]"</pattern>
            <example>data-sync="state:content.sender_name"</example>
        </rule>
    </isomorphism_rules>

    <matrix>

<din_knowledge version="V9" standard="DIN 5008:2020">

  <!-- METADATA & CONTEXT -->
  <meta>
    <file>.brain/03_domain_knowledge.md</file>

  <!-- 1. GLOBAL PAGE DIMENSIONS -->

      <dimension axis="width" value="210" unit="mm" />

    </margins>
  </section>

      <mark type="fold_1" axis="y" value="87" unit="mm" />
    </variant>
    <variant name="Form B">
      <zone type="header" height="45" unit="mm" />
      <coordinate target="address_start" axis="y" value="50" unit="mm" />
      <mark type="fold_1" axis="y" value="105" unit="mm" />
    </variant>
    <common_elements>
      <mark type="hole" axis="y" value="148.5" unit="mm" alignment="center" />
    </common_elements>
  </section>

  <!-- 3. ADDRESS FIELD -->
  <section id="DIN.ADDR" title="The Address Field">
    <container>
      <dimension axis="width" value="85" unit="mm" />
      <dimension axis="height" value="45" unit="mm" />
      <coordinate axis="x" value="20" unit="mm" reference="left_edge" />
    </container>

    <sub_zone type="zone_1" name="Return & Notes">
      <dimension axis="height" value="17.7" unit="mm" />
      <line_count max="5" />
      <content type="return_address">
        <typo_constraint property="font-size" min="6" max="8" unit="pt" />
        <position>Topmost line</position>
      </content>
      <content type="notes">
        <examples>Einschreiben, Nicht nachsenden</examples>
      </content>
    </sub_zone>

    <sub_zone type="zone_2" name="Address">
      <dimension axis="height" value="27.3" unit="mm" />
      <line_count max="6" />
      <typo_constraint property="font-size" min="8" unit="pt" />
      <layout_rule>No empty lines allowed within the address block</layout_rule>

      <international_rules>
        <rule target="country_name">LAST LINE in CAPS (German spelling)</rule>
        <rule target="city_name">CAPS (Local spelling)</rule>
        <rule target="country_code">Forbidden (e.g. no 'B-' or 'I-')</rule>
      </international_rules>
    </sub_zone>
  </section>

  <!-- 4. INFO BLOCK -->
  <section id="DIN.REF" title="The Info Block">
    <positioning>

      <coordinate axis="y" form="A" value="32" unit="mm" />
      <coordinate axis="y" form="B" value="50" unit="mm" />

    <dimension axis="width" max="75" unit="mm" />

  <!-- 5. CONTENT STRUCTURE -->
  <section id="DIN.BODY" title="Content Structure & Layout">

    <element name="Subject" id="subject_line">
      <position reference="address_field_content" offset="2" unit="lines" direction="below" />
      <syntax prefix="forbidden" list="Betreff, Betr., Betrifft" />
      <syntax suffix="forbidden" char="." />
      <style bold="permitted" multiline="allowed" />
    </element>

    <element name="Salutation" id="salutation">
      <position reference="subject_line" offset="2" unit="lines" direction="below" />
    </element>

    <element name="Body Text" id="body">
      <spacing pre_salutation="1" unit="blank_line" />
      <spacing line_height="1.0" />
      <spacing paragraph_sep="1" unit="blank_line" />
    </element>

    <element name="Greeting" id="greeting">
      <position reference="body_text" offset="1" unit="blank_line" direction="below" />
      <syntax comma="optional" />
    </element>

    <element name="Signature Block" id="signature">
      <item name="Company Name">
        <position reference="greeting" offset="1" unit="blank_line" direction="below" />
      </item>
      <item name="Handwritten Space">
        <dimension height="3" unit="blank_lines" />
      </item>
      <item name="Typed Name">
        <position reference="handwritten_space" direction="below" />
        <prefix>i. A. or ppa.</prefix>
      </item>
    </element>

    <element name="Attachments" id="attachments">
      <position variant="standard" reference="typed_name" offset="1" unit="blank_line" direction="below" />
      <position variant="no_signature" reference="text" offset="3" unit="blank_lines" direction="below" />
      <position variant="overflow" coordinate_x="125" unit="mm" align="greeting" />
      <syntax bold="allowed" colon="forbidden">Anlage, Anlagen</syntax>
    </element>

  </section>

  <!-- 6. TYPOGRAPHY & FORMATTING -->
  <section id="DIN.TYPO" title="Typography & Formatting Rules">

    <typo_rule category="Numbers">
      <rule name="grouping" separator="space" method="thousands">
        <example>1 500</example>
        <example>50 000 000</example>
      </rule>
      <rule name="currency" separator="comma" decimals="2">
        <example>25,36 €</example>
        <example>1.573,45 EUR</example>
        <note>Currency codes use dot separators in finance, spaces in text</note>
      </rule>
      <rule name="post_office_box" grouping="pairs_from_right">
        <example>3 22 63</example>
      </rule>
      <rule name="zip_code" grouping="none">
        <example>12345</example>
      </rule>
    </typo_rule>

    <typo_rule category="Dates">
      <format type="ISO 8601" pattern="YYYY-MM-DD" priority="preferred">
        <example>2026-01-09</example>
      </format>
      <format type="Numeric" pattern="DD.MM.YYYY" constraint="leading_zeros_mandatory">
        <example>09.01.2026</example>
      </format>
      <format type="Alphanumeric" pattern="D. Month YYYY">
        <example>9. Januar 2026</example>
      </format>
    </typo_rule>

    <typo_rule category="Phone">
      <format type="Standard" pattern="Area Space Number">
        <example>030 123456</example>
      </format>
      <format type="Extension" separator="hyphen">
        <example>030 123456-12</example>
      </format>
      <format type="International" pattern="+CC Area(no_zero) Number">
        <example>+49 30 123456</example>
      </format>
    </typo_rule>

    <typo_rule category="Abbreviations">
      <rule condition="pronounced_as_words" format="period_space">
        <example>z. B.</example>
        <example>u. a.</example>
      </rule>
      <rule condition="pronounced_as_letters" format="no_space">
        <example>GmbH</example>
        <example>AG</example>
      </rule>
      <rule condition="units" format="space_before">
        <example>10 kg</example>
      </rule>
      <exception>usw., usf.</exception>
    </typo_rule>

    <typo_rule category="Highlighting">
      <allowed>Bold, Size change, Centering, Indent</allowed>
      <discouraged>Underlining</discouraged>
      <forbidden>Spacing out letters (S p e r r e n)</forbidden>
    </typo_rule>

    <typo_rule category="Page Numbering">
      <format location="header_center">- 2 -</format>
      <format location="footer_right">Seite 2 von 3</format>
    </typo_rule>

  </section>

  <!-- 7. ACADEMIC TITLES -->
  <section id="DIN.TITLES" title="Academic & Professional Titles">
    <placement type="Degrees" position="before_name">
      <examples>Dr., Prof.</examples>
    </placement>
    <placement type="Graduates" position="after_name">
      <examples>B.A., M.Sc.</examples>
    </placement>
    <placement type="Functions" position="line_below_name">
      <examples>Director, Vorstand</examples>
    </placement>
  </section>

</din_knowledge>
